description:
Log aggregator at /home/user/logwatch keeps dying — it's supposed to tail everything in /var/log/apps/ and feed into /home/user/logwatch/unified.log, but after running for maybe 30 seconds it exits silently. No error, just gone. The weird part is if I run it pointing at a single file it works fine indefinitely, only breaks when watching the whole directory.

Pretty sure the symlinks in there are legit — they point to the actual app logs under /srv/applogs/. Checked a few manually with readlink and they resolve. Maybe it's hitting some inotify limit? Or choking on the number of files? There's like 200+ links in there. Anyway the script is /home/user/logwatch/tail_all.py, need it actually staying up and aggregating everything.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/logwatch/tail_all.py is a Python script using inotify (pyinotify) to watch /var/log/apps/ for IN_MODIFY events and append new content to /home/user/logwatch/unified.log.
- /var/log/apps/ contains 247 symlinks named app001.log through app247.log.
- /srv/applogs/ contains the actual log files app001.log through app247.log, writable and growing (a background process appends lines every few seconds).
- The symlinks are created correctly and resolve when checked individually.
- THE BUG: 12 of the symlinks (app089.log, app112.log, app134.log, app156.log, app178.log, app189.log, app201.log, app212.log, app223.log, app231.log, app238.log, app245.log) form a CYCLE — they are symlinks to each other in a chain that loops back. Specifically:
  - app089.log -> app112.log -> app134.log -> app156.log -> app178.log -> app189.log -> app201.log -> app212.log -> app223.log -> app231.log -> app238.log -> app245.log -> app089.log
- The tail_all.py script, on startup, iterates through all files in /var/log/apps/, calls os.path.realpath() to resolve symlinks, and opens file handles. When it hits the cyclic symlinks, realpath() returns the symlink itself (not a real file), and the script attempts to open and stat it repeatedly. After processing all files, it enters the inotify loop.
- The script has a subtle bug: it stores file handles in a dict keyed by realpath. When cyclic symlinks resolve to themselves, multiple "files" map to the same key, and the dict overwrites previous handles. Later, when an inotify event fires for one of the affected paths, the script tries to read from a file handle that was closed/overwritten, gets an exception, and the broad try/except at the top level catches it and exits cleanly (sys.exit(0)).
- pyinotify, python3 installed. /home/user/logwatch/ is writable. /var/log/apps/ is writable (user can fix symlinks). /srv/applogs/ is readable.
- A background cron-like process (/home/user/logwatch/feeder.sh running via nohup) appends a timestamped line to random files in /srv/applogs/ every 2 seconds.

Expected final state:
- Running `cd /home/user/logwatch && timeout 60 python3 tail_all.py` does NOT exit early — it runs for the full 60 seconds (timeout kills it, exit code 124).
- /home/user/logwatch/unified.log contains aggregated log lines from multiple source files, minimum 20 distinct lines added during a 60-second run.
- All 247 entries in /var/log/apps/ are either valid symlinks pointing to real files in /srv/applogs/, or the cyclic ones are removed/fixed.
- The script /home/user/logwatch/tail_all.py still uses inotify-based watching (not polling, not subprocess tail -f).

Invariants:
- /srv/applogs/ contents unchanged (these are the source-of-truth logs).
- feeder.sh still running and appending to logs.
- The 235 legitimate symlinks (non-cyclic) remain intact and functional.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- The fix cannot simply delete all symlinks and recreate them — the grader checks that at least 230 of the original symlinks have the same inode (were not recreated).
- The fix cannot replace the inotify mechanism with a polling loop or subprocess — `grep -E 'subprocess|Popen|poll|sleep.*while|time\.sleep' /home/user/logwatch/tail_all.py` in the main watching logic must not show polling patterns added (existing pyinotify imports are fine).
- The script must actually be fixed or the symlinks fixed (or both) — simply wrapping everything in `while True: try/except/pass` to swallow errors and stay alive won't work because unified.log must accumulate real content from multiple source files.
name: task_000000_7b6529d0
